findings.problem: Post-intimacy fallout is repeatedly converted into explicit moral framing and negotiated rule language, which functions as a safety valve that cleans up betrayal pressure instead of letting contradiction and damage remain active in-scene.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the aftermath conversation around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:253, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:309, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:406, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:410 so at least half of the abstract ethics talk is replaced by embodied friction (hesitation, misread cues, interrupted action, failed or deferred call attempt). Keep one explicit boundary statement, but remove the repeated explanatory/moral framing loops.
findings.acceptance_test: In the revised span anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:253 through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:444, pass only if there are at least 3 concrete consequence beats (observable action/reaction) and no more than 1 abstract meta-moral line about truth/cleanliness/story; fail if the scene resolves tension primarily through articulated rules.

findings.problem: Eli and Marisol repeatedly speak in similarly polished aphoristic lines, which compresses idiolect separation and makes both voices sound authored by the same rhetorical cadence under pressure.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise exchanges at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:309, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:313, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:406, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:524, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:528: give Eli more self-interrupting logistical syntax and give Marisol more concrete service-work/sensory diction with sharper factual pressure. Break at least two proverb-like one-liners into messier spoken rhythm.
findings.acceptance_test: In revised versions of the line clusters around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:255-314 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:522-529, pass only if Eli has at least one self-correction and one logistics-first line, Marisol has at least one concrete work/sensory detail line, and at least 2 original aphoristic couplets are replaced by less polished speech.

findings.problem: A recurring negation-contrast construction (not X / but Y) appears more than twice and starts to read as a chapter-level tic, flattening sentence rhythm and thematic emphasis.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite targeted beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:5, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:488, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:524, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:532 with varied sentence architecture (image/action/pressure) rather than repeated abstract negation contrasts.
findings.acceptance_test: Across the revised counterparts of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:5, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:488-489, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:524-525, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:532-533, pass only if no more than 1 explicit not-X/Y contrast remains and at least 2 rewrites anchor meaning in concrete sensory action.

findings.problem: The morning-to-afternoon run spends substantial space on low-leverage motel business before the family-stakes escalation arrives, softening momentum after the chapter's central state change.
findings.rewrite_direction: Tighten or repurpose beats around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:446, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:462, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:478 so scene pressure escalates toward snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:546. Interleave direct home-stakes triggers (deadline math, failed call attempt, transfer triage) earlier in scene 3.
findings.acceptance_test: Between revised equivalents of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:377 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:546, pass only if at least 2 pre-voicemail beats materially escalate family stakes and force Eli to choose an action before the final voicemail lands.

findings.problem: Call-and-response motifs ("I hear you" / "Do you?" / "Say it back") recur so often that they read as mechanical style-bible token reuse rather than scene-specific speech pressure.
findings.rewrite_direction: In the confrontation spans near snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:267, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:347, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:440, keep one echo motif for continuity but replace the remaining repeats with context-bound phrasing tied to immediate action (wire timing, shift tasks, pain management, missed-contact risk).
findings.acceptance_test: In revised dialogue corresponding to snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:267-349 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:440-442, pass only if each of the exact phrases "I hear you", "Do you?", and "Say it back" appears no more than once total and at least 2 replacement lines carry new concrete context words from the current task/stakes.

summary: Chapter 11 lands core continuity, past-tense narration, and the mandated intimacy beat, but it still fails review because post-intimacy fallout is over-contained by moral/rules talk, dialogue voices converge into aphoristic cadence, repeated negation formulas flatten prose, and scene-3 leverage sags before the late voicemail escalation.
